records.ety: [L. impius; pref. im- not + pius piou. See Pious.]
usage: Irreligious is negative, impious and profane are positive. An indifferent man may be irreligious; a profane man is irreverent in speech and conduct; an impious man is wickedly and boldly defiant in the strongest sense. Profane also has the milder sense of secular.

defs: Not pious; wanting piety; irreligious; irreverent; ungodly; profane; wanting in reverence for the Supreme Being; as, an impious deed; impious language.
quotes: When vice prevails, and impious men bear away, The post of honor is a private station.
